subtitleLines: SiClink is building a high-bandwidth, bidirectional I/O,

position.headline: Visual Reconstruction: Making the Impossible Visible
position.body: Through visual decoding and high-precision neural stimulation algorithms, we enable a closed loop of visual reconstruction from perception to write — letting the brain see the world again.

vision.headline: Turning Thought into Signals. Turning Connection into Reality.
bodyLines: SiClink is building a visual interface between the brain and the external world, enabling vision to flow both ways between humans and machines.
We explore how neural activity can be reconstructed as visual information, and how external visual information can support the restoration of visual perception.

modules.title: Bidirectional Flexible Electrodes
modules.body: Bridging the physical interface between silicon and carbon

modules.title: Neural Model Platform
modules.body: Building a one-stop BCI development platform for deployment

bodyLines: Giving every developer a trusted starting point —
where intention reaches, silicon and carbon interconnect.

bodyLines: We welcome explorers bold enough to tackle unsolved challenges.
community.videoAlt: Create with us ambient background video

footer.motto: Accelerating silicon–carbon connectivity. Extending the boundaries of life.

sections.title: Our Beginning
sections.headline: Starting from the "Last Micron"
sections.body: Founded in December 2025, SiClink takes its name from "Silicon-Carbon Link," reflecting its mission to connect electronic systems with biological neural networks. Building on long-term research in ultraflexible neural interfaces, neural stimulation, and visual decoding, SiClink is developing stable, high-bandwidth, bidirectional brain–computer interface systems.

sections.title: Our Values
sections.headline: Drive outcomes with ownership, connect strength through openness, and ensure reliability with rigor.

closing.title: Connect the Future With Us
closing.body: SiClink brings together explorers from neuroscience, hardware, algorithms, software, and product development to build the next generation of brain–computer interface systems.
